subject: [RFC PATCH 03/45] mm: page_alloc: use trylock for PCP lock in free path to avoid lock inversion

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch fixes a deadlock scenario in the Linux kernel's page allocation code by always using trylock for the PCP lock in the free path, rather than blocking on it. This is necessary because the allocation path acquires the PCP lock with interrupts enabled, while other paths may hold locks that are also taken in hardirq context, creating a potential ABBA deadlock. The patch restores the mainline invariant of never blocking on PCP lock acquisition in the free path by using spin_trylock_irqsave() and falling back to the zone buddy when the trylock fails.
